recaps.text: The quest to destroy the One Ring has scattered its companions. In the west, Gandalf, Aragorn, and the riders of Rohan have beaten the traitor-wizard Saruman and now face the far greater threat of the Dark Lord's armies gathering against Gondor. In the east, the creature Gollum betrayed Frodo to a giant spider; Frodo was taken prisoner by orcs, and his servant Sam - who briefly carried the ring - is left outside the tower, determined to save him.

Gandalf and Pippin reach the great city of Minas Tirith and find its Steward, Denethor, sunk in despair. The enemy's host, led by the dreadful Lord of the Nazgûl, lays siege to the city. Rohan's king Théoden musters his riders and comes to Gondor's aid. In the battle on the fields before the walls Théoden falls, but the Nazgûl-lord is slain by the disguised Éowyn and the hobbit Merry, and Aragorn arrives by ship with fresh strength to turn the tide.

The despairing Denethor tries to burn himself and his wounded son Faramir on a pyre, and is stopped only in time to save Faramir. The battle won at heavy cost, the captains take counsel. Knowing Frodo still bears the ring toward the Mountain, they resolve to march the remaining strength of the west to the very gate of Mordor - a desperate feint meant to draw the Enemy's eye away from the two hobbits creeping through his land.

Sam rescues Frodo from the orc-tower, and the two struggle in disguise across the ashen plains of Mordor toward the Mountain of Fire, tormented by thirst and the ring's crushing weight. At the very brink Frodo's will fails and he claims the ring for himself - but Gollum, who has trailed them all along, attacks and bites it from his hand, then topples with it into the fire. The ring is destroyed, and with it the Dark Lord's power is broken forever.

With Sauron's fall his armies break and flee. Aragorn is crowned king of the reunited realm and weds the elf-maiden Arwen; Éowyn and Faramir are healed and betrothed. Honored and rewarded, the hobbits journey home at last, only to find the Shire spoiled and tyrannized in their absence - so they rouse their people and set it right. Merry and Pippin are hailed as heroes and Sam marries and rebuilds, but Frodo, wounded in body and spirit by the burden he bore, cannot find peace, and in the end passes over the sea with the last of the elves, leaving the Shire to those he helped save.
